records.ety: [F. commode, fr. commode convenient, L. commodus; com- + modus measure, mode. See Mode.]

defs: A kind of headdress formerly worn by ladies, raising the hair and fore part of the cap to a great height.
quotes: Or under high commodes, with looks erect.

defs: A piece of furniture, so named according to temporary fashion
A chest of drawers or a bureau.
A night stand with a compartment for holding a chamber vessel.
A movable sink or stand for a wash bowl, with closet.
